data.title: Bitget IPO Prime's first project, preSPAX, is now open for subscription.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Bitget's first IPO Prime project, preSPAX, is now open for subscription, with the deadline for investment being 14:00 (UTC+8) on April 21st. Token allocation will take place from 14:00 to 18:00 (UTC+8) on April 21st; spot trading will commence at 20:00 (UTC+8) on April 21st. More details can be found on the official Bitget platform.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 77,000 USDT with a Narrowed 2.73% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 18, 2026, 07:42 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 77,000 USDT and is now trading at 76,988.960938 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 2.73% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Hyperliquid Whale Positions Reach $3.715 Billion
data.description:
Hyperliquid platform whales currently hold positions totaling $3.715 billion, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass reveals that long positions account for $1.874 billion, representing 50.44% of the total, while short positions amount to $1.841 billion, making up 49.56%. Long positions have seen profits of $46.4198 million, whereas short positions have incurred losses of $68.8451 million.

Notably, a whale address, 0x0ddf..02, has taken a threefold full-margin short position on BTC at a price of $67,992.1, with unrealized losses currently standing at $9.0449 million.

data.title: Polymarket Launches Betting Market on Strait of Hormuz Renaming
data.description: Polymarket has introduced a new betting market focused on whether U.S. President Donald Trump will rename the Strait of Hormuz. According to NS3.AI, the probability of a 'Yes' outcome has decreased to 6%. The market has seen a betting volume surpassing $47,000. The resolution to 'Yes' will occur only if Trump publicly announces the name change by 11:59 PM Eastern Time on May 31, 2026.

data.title: Polymarket Launches Market on Israel-Hezbollah Ceasefire Extension
data.description: Polymarket has introduced a new market focused on the potential extension of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire agreement. According to NS3.AI, as of April 26, the odds of the ceasefire being extended have risen to 78%, a significant increase from the 15% odds recorded on April 21. The total betting volume for this market has reached approximately $36,000.

data.title: Major Banks Write Off $5.6 Billion in Bad Loans Amid Record Consumer Debt
data.description: JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo collectively wrote off $5.606 billion in bad loans during the first quarter of 2026. According to NS3.AI, Federal Reserve data indicates that consumer credit card and other revolving loans at commercial banks hit a record $1.083 trillion in the week ending April 1. Despite these challenges, Jamie Dimon noted that the U.S. economy showed resilience throughout the quarter, even as the list of potential risks continued to grow.

data.title: Polymarket has updated its search query, "How long will the ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah be extended?", with a preliminary probability of 78% as of April 26th.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, a new event on Polymarket, titled "How Long Will the Ceasefire Agreement Between Israel and Hezbollah Be Extended?", shows a 15% probability as of April 21, a 12% decrease over the past 7 days; and a 78% probability as of April 26, a 40% increase over the past 7 days. The total bet on this event is currently around $36,000. The contract rules state that if the 10-day ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah announced on April 16, 2026, is formally extended—defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed-upon extension of direct military contact between Israel and Hezbollah by a specified date (11:59 PM Eastern Time)—the market will mark it as "Yes". Otherwise, the market will ultimately mark it as "No". Extensions to the April 16 ceasefire, as well as new agreements planned to take effect before or at the end of the initial agreement, will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided that no ceasefire is in effect during the ceasefire period. If a qualified agreement is formally reached before the resolution date, the market will judge it as "yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect. An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires explicit public confirmation from both the Israeli government and Hezbollah that they have agreed to cease military hostilities for more than the initially agreed 10-day period, or overwhelming media coverage confirming a formal extension of the ceasefire. Any form of informal understanding, secret communication, de-escalation, or unilateral ceasefire does not meet the criteria unless a confirmed agreement on a conditional extension is reached. Similarly, newly reached humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical halts do not qualify. Newly reached broader peace agreements that include a conditional ceasefire extension or a pause in military conflict qualify. Agreements that list future negotiations or de-escalation measures but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire will not qualify. The market's decision will be based on official statements from the Israeli government and Hezbollah. However, overwhelming media coverage confirming an official ceasefire extension will also be considered. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Polymarket has added a new listing stating that "Trump will rename the Strait of Hormuz to 'Trump Strait' by May 31st," and the probability of a "yes" answer has dropped by 31% in the past 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, a new prediction event has been added to Polymarket: "Trump will rename the Strait of Hormuz to 'Trump Strait' by May 31st." The probability of a "Yes" vote is currently 6%, down 31% in the last 24 hours. There are currently over $47,000 in bets on this event. The contract rules are: if Trump publicly announces that the United States will officially name the Strait of Hormuz "Trump Strait" or any equivalent name containing "Trump" by 11:59 PM Eastern Time on May 31, 2026, the market will decide to support "Yes." Otherwise, the market will settle for "No." The primary solution for this market will be official information from Donald Trump, but consensus based on credible reports is also possible. Odaily Seer continues to monitor prediction markets, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Nvidia and OpenAI Invest $20 Billion Each in AI Chip Market
data.description: Nvidia and OpenAI have each committed $20 billion to advance their positions in the AI inference chip market. According to PANews, this significant investment marks a strategic move in the ongoing competition for dominance in AI computing power. Meanwhile, Cerebras has filed for an IPO, valuing the company at $35 billion. This silent battle for control over the future of AI computing is reshaping the landscape of the technology market, which is valued at hundreds of billions of dollars.

data.title: Iran Rejects Option to Move Enriched Uranium Abroad
data.description: On April 18, Iran's Foreign Ministry announced that the option to transfer Iran's enriched uranium to another country has been dismissed. According to BlockBeats, this decision marks a significant stance in Iran's nuclear policy. The move comes amid ongoing discussions and international scrutiny over Iran's nuclear activities.

data.title: British Officials Highlight Impact of Iran Conflict on US Relations
data.description: British officials attending the IMF and World Bank spring meetings have emphasized the enduring impact of the Iran conflict on the United Kingdom's relationship with the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that the discussions at the meetings underscored the geopolitical tensions and their implications for international diplomacy. The conflict has strained ties between the two nations, with British representatives expressing concerns over future diplomatic engagements and cooperation. The meetings served as a platform for officials to address these challenges and explore potential avenues for mitigating the conflict's effects on bilateral relations.

data.title: Iran reopens some airspace
data.description: Iran's Civil Aviation Organization announced the reopening of some of Iran's airspace. (Xinhua)

data.title: Rumen Radev's Party Leads Polls Ahead of Bulgarian Election
data.description: Rumen Radev, known for his critical stance towards the European Union and his outreach to Russia, is seeing his new political party leading in the polls ahead of Bulgaria's upcoming election on Sunday. Bloomberg posted on X that Radev's party is gaining significant traction among voters, reflecting a shift in the political landscape of the country. As Bulgaria prepares for the election, the influence of Radev's party could signal changes in the nation's approach to both EU relations and its foreign policy direction.

data.title: Iran Reopens Portions of Airspace
data.description: On April 18, Iran's Civil Aviation Organization announced the reopening of certain areas of its airspace. According to BlockBeats, this decision marks a significant development in the region's aviation sector. The reopening is expected to facilitate increased air traffic and improve connectivity for international flights. The move comes after previous restrictions were imposed due to various geopolitical and security concerns. The organization has not specified which areas are affected by this change, but it is anticipated to have a positive impact on the country's aviation industry.

data.title: Putin's special envoy: US waivers on Russian oil will affect more than 100 million barrels of oil en route.
data.description: Market news: A special envoy of Russian President Vladimir Putin stated that the US waiver for Russian oil will affect more than 100 million barrels of oil already in transit. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Exemption on Russian Oil to Impact Over 100 Million Barrels
data.description: On April 18, market reports indicate that a statement from a special envoy of Russian President Vladimir Putin suggests that the U.S. exemption on Russian oil will affect more than 100 million barrels of oil in transit. According to BlockBeats, this development could have significant implications for the global oil market.

data.title: AI Sparks Awakening in Gen Z and Alpha Generations, Bitcoin Seen as Wealth Storage Tool
data.description: AI is emerging as a catalyst for awakening among Generation Z and Alpha, with Bitcoin anticipated to serve as a tool for wealth storage. According to PANews, the influence of artificial intelligence is reshaping perspectives and financial strategies for these younger generations. As they become more attuned to technological advancements, Bitcoin is increasingly viewed as a viable option for preserving wealth. This shift highlights the growing intersection between digital currencies and generational trends, underscoring the transformative impact of AI on financial behaviors.

data.title: Ethereum ETFs See Continued Inflows, Led by Fidelity's FETH
data.description: U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs experienced net inflows totaling approximately $127.4 million on April 17, marking the seventh consecutive day of positive flows. According to NS3.AI, data from Farside Investors indicated that Fidelity's FETH was the leading contributor, with $84.1 million in inflows.

data.title: Anthropic's Next Funding Round Probabilities on Polymarket
data.description:
Polymarket has introduced a new event contract regarding the timeline for Anthropic's next funding round. According to Odaily, the probability of completion by the end of June is currently at 22%, while by the end of December, it stands at 81%.

The contract stipulates that if Anthropic publicly and officially announces the completion of its next funding round by the specified date (Eastern Time), the market will resolve as 'Yes.' Otherwise, it will resolve as 'No.' Qualified announcements must clearly confirm the completion of the new funding round and can be made through official channels such as press releases, investor announcements, regulatory filings, or consensus reports from credible media. Informal announcements, anonymous sources, or leaks do not qualify. If Anthropic is unable to complete the funding round due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by another entity, the market will resolve as 'No.' The primary settlement sources for this market will be Anthropic's official announcements and documents, such as SEC filings, though credible consensus reports may also be used.
